food_subgroup: Fast food burger meal with sides (burger, large fries, large soft drink)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general hamburger/cheeseburger entry; no specific double cheeseburger meal entry exists. FOODON_00002383 used as closest defensible anchor for the burger component.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers hamburgers and cheeseburgers as a category; no specific double cheeseburger upsized meal code exists. Used as closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for double cheeseburger meal; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (double cheeseburger + large fries + large soft drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of a double cheeseburger (approximately 200 g), a large portion of french fries (approximately 170 g), and a large soft drink (approximately 280 g / 280 ml), totalling approximately 650 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — Double Cheeseburger, Large Fries, Large Coca-Cola; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cheeseburger (double patty), french fries (fast food), and cola beverage
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Singapore (primary menu nutrition data); USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (cross-reference validation)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values per 100 g are derived by combining McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data for the Double Cheeseburger (200 g, ~490 kcal), Large Fries (170 g, ~490 kcal), and Large Coca-Cola (280 ml, ~112 kcal) into a composite meal of ~650 g total weight. Per-100 g values are calculated from the weighted composite. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries (NDB 21108 fast-food cheeseburger double patty; NDB 21137 fast-food french fries; NDB 14400 cola beverage) were used to validate macro ratios and micronutrient estimates. Where McDonald's Singapore data and USDA data diverged, McDonald's Singapore published values were used as primary.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — fast food burger and refined white bread bun composite estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a McDonald's Double Cheeseburger Upsized Meal as a composite. GI of 65 is estimated as a weighted composite: the glazed white-flour bun and french fries components carry GI ~72 (white bread reference, University of Sydney GI Database); the beef patty and cheese contribute negligible glycemic load. The cola component (GI ~63, University of Sydney GI Database, glucose-sweetened cola) is included in the composite weighting. Weighted average across carbohydrate-contributing components yields an estimated meal GI of approximately 65 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (65 × 143 g carbohydrate in 650 g meal) / 100 ≈ 93.0. This is a high glycemic load meal.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~2015 mg per meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single upsized double cheeseburger meal provides approximately 2015 mg sodium, exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption of this meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~93 per meal) and high refined carbohydrate content from bun, fries, and cola
type2_diabetes.guidance: The upsized meal delivers approximately 143 g of carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~93, causing a substantial postprandial glucose spike; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal or substitute the cola with water and the large fries with a side salad to substantially reduce glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~15.6 g per meal), trans fat (~1.3 g per meal), sodium (~2015 mg per meal), and high total energy (~1086 kcal per meal)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of high saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and total caloric density in this meal is associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and blood pressure; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption and consider lower-fat, lower-sodium alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Double Cheeseburger Upsized Meal is a fast food combo meal served at McDonald's Singapore, consisting of a double cheeseburger, a large portion of french fries, and a large soft drink (typically Coca-Cola). The double cheeseburger features two grilled beef patties layered with processed American-style cheese slices, caramelised onions, and condiments, all enclosed in a soft glazed white-flour bun. The fries are deep-fried in vegetable oil and lightly salted. The meal is halal-certified in Singapore. Nutritionally, this is a high-energy, high-sodium, high-saturated-fat meal with a high glycemic load. A single upsized serving (~650 g) provides approximately 1086 kcal, 143 g carbohydrate, 40 g fat, and over 2000 mg sodium. The meal is classified as ultra-processed and is not recommended for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The high refined carbohydrate content from the bun, fries, and cola drives a substantial postprandial glucose response (estimated GI 65, GL ~93 per meal). The meal is widely consumed in Singapore as a quick lunch or dinner option and is available at McDonald's outlets across the island. It is listed on field survey data as a delivery item.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a McDonald's Double Cheeseburger Upsized Meal?
Is the Double Cheeseburger Upsized Meal halal?
How much sodium is in a McDonald's upsized meal?
Is a double cheeseburger meal suitable for diabetics?
What is the glycemic index of a McDonald's burger meal?
How much fat is in a McDonald's Double Cheeseburger Meal?
Can I eat a double cheeseburger meal if I have high blood pressure?
What are the macros for a McDonald's upsized meal Singapore?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from a composite calculation using McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data (primary) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (cross-reference). McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data but not per-100 g values; the per-100 g figures here are calculated from the weighted composite. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database reference foods. No single approved primary source provides exact per-100 g data for this specific composite meal, hence confidence is medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in McDonald's Singapore nutrition data or the USDA SR Legacy entries used for cross-reference validation. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary or cross-reference sources used. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary or cross-reference sources used. 4. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous entries and should be treated as approximate. 5. Serving size of 650 g is an estimate based on McDonald's Singapore published item weights: Double Cheeseburger ~200 g, Large Fries ~170 g, Large Coca-Cola ~280 ml. 6. The meal is halal-certified at McDonald's Singapore outlets. 7. GI of 65 is an analogous composite estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific meal exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 8. Glycemic load per serving of 93.0 is calculated as (65 × 143) / 100, where 143 g is the estimated total carbohydrate in the 650 g meal serving. This represents a very high glycemic load. 9. The food_id GD000136 is from the field survey data seed dataset and is not present in the FD series food index.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed composite per-100g nutrient values from McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cross-reference.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit source-absence justification in notes.
Provided analogous GI estimate from University of Sydney GI Database with full calculation methodology.
Added multilingual local names in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy G6 for Singapore-served food.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list for perfect consistency.
Populated all three required health context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
Wrote natural_language_description exceeding 200 characters with full clinical and cultural context.
Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' to comply with allowed enum values, resolving G5 hard gate failure.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
